name: Mattie Lois Albert, A.B.
description: Mattie Lois is one of our quieter girls, yet she is a loyal supporter of Howard's various activities. She always has a smile and consequently many friends, who regret to see her leave.

name: Richard L. Alexander, A.B.
clubs: Advisor to Faculty from Divinity Club (4)
Honor Fellowship New York School of Sociology
description: Mr. Alexander is the type of man that Howard will be proud to point out as one of her graduates. His keen intellect and good-natured disposition have made their impressions on our campus.

name: Ernest E. Allen, A.B.
description: In his four years here Ernest has found time to be an outstanding member of our band and to edit the Crimson successfully. He is one of Howard's steadiest and most capable students.

name: Elna Almgren, A.B.
description: Elna is one of the few co-eds who has remained true to long hair. Her sunny disposition has made her many friends at ol' Howard.

name: B. F. Atkins, A.B.
clubs: President of the Franklin Literary Society
description: Frank started in ahead of our class, but we're mighty glad that he chose the class of '26 to graduate. He has a heart that must fill up his big body and lots of personality that he hands out with his magnetic smile. His tenor voice has been a great attraction of the Glee Club and their successful season of '25 may be credited to him.

name: Marion Atkinson
description: Marion's brown eyes and smiling face have made her many friends. She is one of Howard's staunchest co-eds and we know that she will make her mark in the world. With her go the good wishes of all the students.

name: J. E. Bains
description: "Snake" has been one of the mainstays of the Crimson-Blue in baseball and basketball. His pitching in the spring and his guarding and goal shooting in the winter are some of the reasons Southern hasn't walked away with Howard in the last four years. He hasn't let his athletics interfere with his campus courses and is quite the Shiek.

name: William T. Bains
description: Bill has been in Howard off and on and we're mighty glad he waited for the class of '26. Although a Pre-Med and spending much of his time in Smith Hall, he has found time to make a letter in baseball. He was also one of the first to help organize a Tennis Club in '22 and '23.

name: Paul Barnett
clubs: Business Manager Y. M. C. A. Handbook (3)
description: To list Paul's honors sounds like a list of the student activities. If there were a "C" in his name we'd know it stood for capability. Add to this his pleasing personality and we cease to wonder why he was such a well-liked student body president. He is a winner of the Birmingham News Scholarship and his "A's" along with his numerous activities show that he highly deserved it.

name: Helen Bass
description: Helen is a girl of unique personality. She possesses rare poise, refinement and congeniality, and has a wide circle of friends.

name: Bennie T. Beckham, A.B.
description: Bennie is a conscientious student, always looking to the right. He is very quiet and a bit hard to know, yet one can't help liking him. He is a consistent worker and always interested in Howard campus life.

name: Robert Hardie Bradford, A.B.
description: Bob doesn't have much to say, but when he does say something it's certainly worth-while. He is a pleasant, accommodating chap, yet serious enough to be one of the most popular fellows in the class of '26.

name: Douglas McKinley Braswell, A.B.
description: Doug is our "extra-super-fine" musician. We're mighty glad that he is lacking in the artistic temperament though, for he has made us a dandy good cheer leader, too. No one will be more sincerely missed on Howard campus than Doug.

name: James L. Campbell, A.B.
description: Jimmie can't decide whether he came to Howard to study chemistry or court the ladies. We think his steady work and winning smile have brought him great success in both lines and speak well for the future. If you want the latest joke—ask Jimmie!

name: Mary Olive Carnley, A.B.
clubs: Delegate Y. W. A. Camp at Ridgecrest (3)
description: Mary Olive came to us in her Junior year from Judson, and we're mighty glad she did, for she is a girl of sterling worth and literary ability. Howard can justly be proud to call her her daughter.

name: Ben F. Causey
description: Ben is "six foot two in his stocking feet" and one of the best hearted fellows we know. He is a very likeable boy and a good, steady student.

name: Mamie Chamblee
description: Mamie is a quiet, sensible girl and quite worth the knowing; a staunch friend and a hard working student.

name: Nannie B. Chandler
clubs: Accompanist Boys' Glee Club and Orchestra (3)
description: When it comes to singing and playing, Nannie B. is a regular somebody. She has played and sung her way into all our hearts and has also had time enough for campus activities and to make the Senior Honor Society for Girls.

name: Margaret Ann Church
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Biology Club
description: Margaret deserted us for Randolph-Macon one year, but knowing a good school she could not resist the temptation and came back to Howard to graduate. We are certainly glad she did, for she is a girl of rare friendliness and charm, blended with a depth of spirit and intellectual ability that makes us proud to call her a friend.

name: William Allen Conner
description: Conner has been with us all four years and has not wasted his time. He is one of the "divines" and his keen intellect predicts a great future for him.

name: Margaret Elizabeth Cox
description: What the rest of us lack in dignity, Margaret makes up. She is a sincere friend with a true and loyal spirit. Her individuality makes a lasting impression upon all with whom she comes in contact.

name: Erman L. Crew
motto: "If you've never heard Erman's cymbals then you've never heard our band!"
description: He's an excellent student, conscientious in his work and an all-round boy.

name: Rena Cruce
description: Rena is a "true blue" girl. Her smile is for everyone and her sympathetic nature brings to her many tales of the woes of others. She has spent much of her time in the library and her friendly charm has led many green rats and upperclassmen to realize the great value of books.

name: Eugene Dawson
description: "Gene" captured our hearts his first year and has during his four years been loved by boys and girls alike. He has made an excellent cheer leader, captained the baseball team, and was president of his class for two years, as well as being elected the most original boy in college. All of which go to prove his all-round qualities and explain his popularity among us.

name: Arthur De Loach
description: Arthur came to us from Newton and since coming to Howard has concentrated his efforts on hard work. He has won many friends in his two years with us, who predict an enviable record for him.

name: Harry E. Dickinson
description: Harry is one of our most popular ministerial students—one who has shown his interest in every phase of college life. Men of his calibre are an exception rather than the rule.

name: Elsie Hope Dillon
description: Elsie is the superior type of girl who possesses unusual ability and intellect. She is one of the few girls who were so outstanding that they were selected for membership in both of the co-ed honor sororities. We foresee great things for her.

name: Hazel Dismukes
description: Hazel is a quiet girl who attends strictly to her own business. Nevertheless, to those who know her, she has a charming personality and is a faithful student.

name: Louise Pollard Douglas
description: Louise has the honor of finishing in three years. She is a rising artist in dramatics and possesses great ability in this line. A charming lisp in her voice makes her irresistible to whomever listens to her.

name: Wilburn B. Douglas
description: "Doug" is one of those too few souls who tends strictly to his own business. He is well liked by everyone and has a liberal supply of friends who will hate to see him leave this year.

name: Mozelle Duncan
description: Mozelle hasn't been with us in regular session, but has come to summer school and on Saturdays to get her degree. She is quiet and demure and we're sorry we haven't had the chance to know her better.

name: Burlie Key Dutton
description: Although Burlie took "time out" to get married, she did not let it interfere with her education, nor has she let anything interfere with her receiving her diploma. She has used her time in school usefully, and anyone will do well to follow in her footsteps.

name: Ernestine Dyer
description: Ernestine is quite a nomad, having migrated to Howard from Central College via Judson. We're glad she stopped with us long enough to graduate. Her stellar work on the basketball court and her amiability have won for her a large circle of friends.

name: Robert C. Eason
description: Bob has gone about his way quietly, but with this same quietness has wound his way into the hearts of many students. Such a persevering worker is bound to succeed in life.

name: Major Watt Espy
description: Major is a steady, consistent student. He doesn't talk very much, but his smile says quite a bit. Although he hasn't made his letters, he has played football, baseball and incidentally a cornet in Howard's million dollar band.

name: John E. Evans
description: Evans is not the kind who goes in for show and talk, but in his unassuming way he accomplishes much. We wish him much success.

name: Floyd W. Faulkner
description: Floyd is one of our most eminent science students. He is a steady, consistent worker and leaves an enviable record behind him.

name: John Lindsey Finklea
description: John is a true type of Southern gentleman. He is a whole-hearted, sincere individual, with a remarkable sense of executive ability. We foretell success for John in the business world.

name: Julia Finklea
description: A demure damsel who might have lived generations ago when "Knighthood was in Flower." Julia has that quality of reserve about her that is so admired by all and so seldom found in the modern girl. She possesses an air of individuality that is thoroughly captivating. Long live the Queen!

name: Lessie Fitts
description: Lessie is an earnest girl that may always be found the same true friend. She doesn't have much to say, but her spirit of graciousness and sweetness have won her a place in our hearts.

name: Fox, Oscar M.
description: Fox is one of the most silent members of the Senior Class. Nevertheless, still waters run deep and we're sure that when he isn't busy with pastorate work, if his thoughts were put in words they would be quite worth hearing.

name: Franklin, Lena
description: Lena is a quiet, demure girl and doesn't spend much time loitering. Consequently she is not as well-known as some of our co-eds. She is worth seeking out and is a staunch supporter of Howard.

name: Freeman, Fred Clemons
description: Though seemingly the embodiment of dignity, Fred was seen "strutting his stuff" as drum major last year. In his Senior year he had the honor of being Manager of the Football Team. Fred is a boy of fine principle and one that can be depended upon.

name: Gaines, Luther
description: Luther has worked hard while at Howard and so won the admiration of students and professors. He is a ministerial student and his keen intellect foretells for him great things.

name: Gardner, Roy L.
description: Roy has been with us all four years, and has interested himself mostly in religious affairs. He has worked hard and success is bound to come.

name: Rosalie Gilbert
description: Rosalie has that "snappy" buoyant way with her and is never still. She can always be depended upon to have her "say." Her energetic nature has helped her to receive her diploma before her four years had been passed at Howard.

name: Gertrude Graves
description: Gertrude is another of our Titian-haired, pleasant, smiling lassies. However, pleasing personality isn't the only item in which she rates an A. She has a head full of brains and comes out on top in all her classes. Such efficiency as hers merits great reward, and we're sure success will not o'erlook her.

name: Jewel Graves
description: Jewel has truly been a "Jewel" to Howard College. To list her honors would take a long time. She is the capable editor of this year's annual, and has always participated actively in all activities on the campus. She is a girl of remarkable vision, a brilliant mind, and loving, sincere disposition. We need not wish her success. She is bound to win it!

name: Roy Curtis Green
description: You have to be busy yourself to find "Greenie," as he believes in work and has very little time for anything else. If application to a task means anything, he is assured of success in after life. Luck to you!

name: Lula Gunn
description: Lula is the kind of girl that one would like to confide all of one's secrets to, knowing that they would be kept as sacred. She takes everything that comes up with a good-natured smile and has endeared herself to all who know her by her devout, Christian life.

name: Willie Mae Hardy
clubs: Delegate S. V. Conference at Indianapolis (2)
description: "Bill" is one of Howard's most popular co-eds. She has taken part in all affairs at Howard and was president of Y. W. C. A. in her Senior year. Add to her other qualities that of going with two boys of the same "frat" and getting away with it! Her place will be hard to fill after she leaves.

name: James Marlin Harris
clubs: Senior Class Editor for Entre Nous (4)
description: Marlin's original wit and humor are always the life of the party. However, underneath this exterior of frivolity lies good common sense and ability. Everybody likes Marlin, and she likes everybody. She has a pleasant disposition and worlds of that overworked word, personality. Although her studies never seem to interfere with her other activities, she has never let her activities keep her from ranking high in her studies. Here's to you "Jimmie!"

name: Curry Haynes
description: This is a boy whose real worth cannot be fully appreciated. He is a steady, staunch supporter of the college. A combination of good common sense with a delicate vein of humor leads everyone who knows him to like him.

name: Charles Aubrey Hearn
description: Aubrey came to college with a purpose—to make good. And if his three years here can be taken as an indication, he has made good, and will continue to do so, for he is an untiring worker.

name: Claude Hearn
description: Claude possesses rare friendliness and may always be found with a smile on his face. He has found time to fall in love and yet graduate in three years. His amiability will win a way in the world.

name: H. S. Higdon
description: A ministerial student of the highest type. He has a dignified reserve which everyone admires and we prophesy great success for him in his future life.

name: Kelloray Hill
description: We are glad that Kelloray came back to Howard to finish after deserting us for a year. Although we think she has a weakness for an "Irish Man" we are sure that this is her only "failing." She is truly loved by everyone.

name: Evelyn Hix
description: Evelyn is one of our "literary lights" and may always be found with a book in her hand. She was one of the charter members of the Girls' Literary Sorority and has always been a good student. "Hix" is a good sport and has world's of executive ability that can put anything across.

name: Julia Beth Hrabowski
description: Having to stop and spell her last name throughout her college days did not seem to phase Julia Beth. She is a girl of unique personality and another of our long-haired co-eds. We believe she will successfully follow art as a vocation after graduation.

name: Carl E. Hyche
description: Carl is an example of the saying that "still water runs deep." He is very quiet, but when called on, never fails to deliver the goods—ask the profs!

name: C. Thaddeus Ivey
description: Thaddeus has loads of executive ability, a depth of sincerity and earnest application to every duty. This combined with thoughtfulness and dignity makes him the ideal Senior Class president.

name: Edward Gray Jackson
clubs: Assistant Business Manager of the Crimson (1)
description: "Jack" is one of the main reasons why the Entre-Nous of last year and this year has been such a success. This boy puts across anything he undertakes, and has been a pillar to the Class of '26.

name: Tom C. Kelly
description: Tom has been a mainstay of the Dramatic and Glee Clubs. Along with this he has found time to be one of the outstanding members of the Y. M. C. A., Alpha Gamma Epsilon Literary Fraternity, and to finish in three years. His tenor voice, amiability and clever art will always recall pleasant memories of a boy without whom the class would have been incomplete.

name: Andrew Hendricks Knight
description: "Hank" is a quiet, unassuming, diligent member of the Class of '26. He is the kind of man whom one admires and respects, and he has but to follow his ideals to make the world sit up and take notice. He plays both football and basketball, and is active in all college activities.

name: Nannie Hope Lee
enrolltime: finish with the Class of '26 instead of waiting for her own
description: Hope has stepped up to finish with the Class of '26 instead of waiting for her own. She is a hard working, conscientious student and her thoughtfulness of others makes her a friend indeed.

name: Harrell Walton Little
description: You'll always find Little quietly going about minding his own business and always passing his work. There are a great many things in store for this boy as he has been a great force in every activity that he has entered.

name: Lois Agnes Majors
description: Lois is one of our attractive brunettes with a sunny disposition. She takes a deep interest in everything relating to Howard, especially basketball. We feel sure that Lois will make her mark in the world.

name: Aubrey Miree, Jr.
description: Aubrey is another of our prominent musicians—and he is said to blow a "mean" saxophone. He is good company at any time and we know that he will make good.

name: Albert Graham Moseley, Jr.
description: Although a bit of a cynic and inclined toward sarcasm at times, Graham's streak of mischief is irresistible to all who know him. He has become part of the Science Hall, as a great part of his time is spent there. Although we have only known him two years we are mighty glad that he decided to enter Howard in his Junior year.

name: Alton Murphy
clubs: Delegate to National Council of S. V. M. at Yonkers, N. Y. (3)
Council Member of S. V. M. at Chicago, Ill. (4)
Treasurer Alabama State Student Volunteer Union (4)
description: Alton is a student with a brilliant mind and far-reaching vision. He is faithful and sincere in all his duties and he is always ready to work for Howard's good. This spirit of helpfulness and his friendly ways have won him many friends.

name: Everett Goodrich McDonough
description: Everett is a pre-med and has spent most of his four years in Smith Hall. He knows about all there is to know about science and bids fair to win fame along this line. The bits of time he spends outside the laboratory have been enough for us to find out his true value and to wish that he had had time to partake freely of all student activities.

name: Nell Virginia McMurry
description: Nell is a girl of dignified mien and looks just like a Senior. She has, however, a most pleasing disposition and is worth the knowing. Nell is so conscientious that we know she will always be found doing her duty.

name: Mary Frances McNeil
description: Frances stars in both journalistic lines and basketball. She is a member of both Hypatia and Chi Delta Phi honorary sororities, which speak of her well-rounded campus life. She is an all-round girl and truly a fine representative of Howard.

name: Frances Newman
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Freshman Commission
Under-Graduate Representative Y. W. C. A.
description: "Dutchie" is one of the best "Y" workers and is quite indispensable to the cabinet. She is a girl of fine ideals and of strong convictions, a true sport and a "peach of a girl." Everyone hates to see her leave.

name: Lucian Newman
description: Lucian is an easy going sort of fellow, and a boy that all like. He is whole-hearted and earnest, and although quiet at times, he does not have to talk very much to make an impression.

name: Paul Oden
description: Overcoming the handicap of blindness with remarkable cheerfulness and brilliancy, Paul has endeared himself to the students of Howard. He is of a very independent and lovable disposition. Would that there were many more like him. Here's to you, Paul!

name: Grace Katherine Pass
clubs: Sophomore Representative to Woman's Student Government
description: Grace never has a "grouch," but may always be found with a jolly laugh and a funny story. She has a quick and ready wit that has made her well liked by all. Added to this is her ability of making good grades at all times.

name: Charles E. Petty
description: "Charlie" will be a figure in the business world some day. He is guided by a high sense of integrity and an alert "get up and get" spirit. He hails from Cullman and doesn't care who knows it. Columbia's gain next year will be Howard's loss.

name: Edgar M. Poe
description: Edgar is truly one of our most brilliant students. He is never too busy to discuss philosophy or any deep subject. He has more than likely gotten more out of his studies than anyone during his four years. We predict that he will be a noted author and philosopher some day.

name: Everett Poole
description: Everett is one of our "budding" poets. We have not found out the inspiration of all this poetry; if he is in love he has concealed it. He is a studious, whole-hearted boy, who has left his mark on the campus without any noise.

name: Martha Isabel Presley
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Woman's Student Government (3)
description: Although Martha has been with us only one year, she has had time to make friends with all Howardites. Her cheery smile and pleasant ways have been quite an addition to Howard's campus. Our only regret is that she did not spend her entire college career here.

name: Condie Sheppard Pugh
description: Condie is a genial, "happy go lucky" product of old Kentucky, who can strum a ukelele, court the ladies, wear extreme fashions, and pass his studies with the same ease of accomplishment. His ready smile will be missed by everyone.

name: Crisler B. Ransom
description: "Chris" is one of our handsomest members, but has managed to stay shy of the ladies. He steadily goes on his own way and is always efficient in his studies.

name: Pauline Ray
description: Pauline is a "ray" of sunshine—possessing rare dramatic talent. She always responds graciously when called upon to "read," and is always ready to help. During her years here she has been one of Howard's best liked co-eds, her gentle manner and refinement being some of her chief assets.

name: Myrtle Reeves
description: Myrtle is a girl of invariable good humor and is generally found with her inseparable friend, "Katie"—a very good combination.

name: Scokel, Paul William
description: "Polly's" smile is almost as famous as his ability at making touchdowns. He is an all-round athlete and his stellar work on the football team has been a great joy to all Howard students and supporters. There is probably no better liked boy on the campus.

name: Shelton, Arnice
description: Arnice is one of our consistent students who always make the honor roll. She is smart without boasting of it, and although very unassuming, she has made a record here that will never be forgotten by the professors or students.

name: Shelton, Roy L.
clubs: President College B. Y. P. U.
description: Roy has always made it his business to speak to everyone. He has constantly made good grades and has been actively connected with college life. You will always find Roy busy.

name: Smith, Katie Elizabeth
clubs: Director of B. Y. P. U. work at West Woodlawn Baptist
Secretary of Junior and Intermediate B. Y. P. U. of Birmingham Association
description: Katie is a girl deeply interested in religious work and plans to do field work for the B. Y. P. U. after she graduates. We know that she will be of service in any branch of work she enters, as she has been a force in every activity she has entered at Howard.

name: Speir, H. Philip
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Howard Scientific Society (4)
description: Speir is one of the campus sheiks—that is, if one may be judged by his attendance upon the ladies. Being friendly and witty, he is rather entertaining, and with all one of the most popular members of the Class of '26.

name: Naufleet Sudduth
description: "Fleet" has a versatile nature. This girl of the Titian hair is a talented musician and plans to do great things with her music. She is an unusual girl and has retained all her friends during her four years here.

name: James Alton Timmerman
clubs: Secretary and Chairman of House Committee of Divinity Club (4)
description: One of the hardest workers in the Senior Class, Timmerman has friends among the students and professors alike. His amiability and sincerity make him an outstanding young preacher.

name: Katherine Tolson
description: "Kat" went to Judson three years, then decided to get her diploma at the best college in the world. Although she hasn't been here long, she has made a way into everyone's heart and we only wish she could have been here longer.

name: Gussie Upchurch
description: Gussie is a great Sunday School worker and a girl of true scholastic spirit. We do not know what she plans to do in the future—but we do know that Howard will always be proud of her.

name: Mary Upchurch
description: Mary had already received her "A.B." before coming to Howard, but came here to take a special course. She is so tiny and childish looking that one can hardly realize that she has so much knowledge in her brain. She is from Clanton, and is proud of it! We all wish there were more Mary's!

name: L. G. Walker
description: L. G. is a boy full of knowledge and he should be, as he has spent most of his time in the library. You would have to search far to find a more conscientious student.

name: Rayburn L. Wear
description: Rayburn we understand "trips the light fantastic toe" in a most enviable way. Although he has all the appearances of the modern "sheik," he is an all-round boy and a true friend.

name: Huey T. Wells
description: We just can't imagine what Howard will be like after "Spike" has gone. He is probably the only boy on the campus without a single enemy. Everyone likes him, and he is one of the highest spots on the horizon of our acquaintance. He is one of our best athletes, playing varsity football and being captain of the basketball team.

name: John J. West
description: West is a student who spends so little of his time on the campus that he is not very well known. However, those who keep on his trail long enough to know him assure us that there is a spiritual and intellectual depth beneath his calm exterior quite worth delving for.

name: Forgus E. Williams
description: "Goose" has certainly deserved his diploma, as he has worked his way at night so that he might have an education. His work on the football field has been unsurpassed. We are for you, "Goose."

name: J. D. Wyatt
description: Coming from a family pre-eminent in ministerial work, "Preacher" Wyatt is not the least of the stars. Although he spends most of his time in outside pastorate work, he is always a willing worker, ready to serve when called upon. Howard is justly proud of such an earnest Christian worker and student.

name: Alice Vines
description: Alice is a blond that is not at all fickle in her disposition. She has a steady aim in view and we know she will attain it. Alice has a large circle of friends that will always follow with interest whatever she does. We imagine she will make a "Cracker-Jack" teacher.

name: Carroll, L. Earl
clubs: Assistant Director College B. Y. P. U. (3)

name: TILLEY, GRACE
description: North Carolina College (1).

name: AWTREY, BIVIEN
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Freshman Class

name: ARTHUR DIX
clubs: Member Y. M. C. A. State Student Executive Council (2)

name: Clarietta Kendricks
description: Woman's College (1).

name: MARTHA EUGENIA PERRY
description: Voted Prettiest Girl (1).

name: KATHLEEN WRIGHT
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Y. W. A.

name: MARGARET BASS
clubs: Maid of Howard-Birmingham-Southern Freshman Football Game

name: LUCY LEA
description: Handwritten note next to name: FEHLER.

name: John Robert Rushing
description: Birmingham News Scholarship Winner.
